item.title: Museum day
item.description:
Thursday, April 2, 2026 at 12 PM

Location: Seattle & Tacoma

Event details

item.title: Swing dance
item.description:
Thursday, April 2, 2026 at 12 PM

Location: https://www.google.com/maps/search/?api=1&query=Hyatt%20Regency%20Bellevue%2C%20900%20Bellevue%20Way%20NE%2C%20Bellevue%2C%20WA%2098004

Bellevue (9.2 miles E) Easter Swing is an annual weekend of competitions, workshops, and social dances at Hyatt Regency Bellevue, 900 Bellevue Way NE in Bellevue.

Event details

item.title: Science fiction & fantasy convention
item.description:
Thursday, April 2, 2026 at 12 PM

Location: https://www.google.com/maps/search/?api=1&query=DoubleTree%20Hotel%20Seattle%20Airport%2C%2018740%20International%20Blvd%20%2C%20SeaTac%2C%20WA%2098188

SeaTac (15 miles S) Norwescon features 200 vendors, panelists, events, game playing, dances, and a masquerade contest at DoubleTree Hotel Seattle Airport, 18740 International Blvd. in SeaTac.

Event details

item.title: Anime festival
item.description:
Friday, April 3, 2026 at 8 AM

Location: https://www.google.com/maps/search/?api=1&query=Seattle%20Convention%20Center%2C%20705%20Pike%20St%2C%20Seattle%2C%20WA%2098101

Downtown (0.2 miles E) Anime is a style of Japanese film and TV animation featured at Sakura-Con, plus gaming, cosplay, cultural panels, dances, concerts, exhibits, and industry guests at Seattle Convention Center, 705 Pike St. in Seattle.

Event details

item.title: Small-town Easter parade
item.description:
Saturday, April 4, 2026 at 10 AM

Location: https://www.google.com/maps/search/?api=1&query=First%20Street%2C%201st%20St%2C%20Snohomish%2C%20WA%2098290

Snohomish (29 miles NE) Billed as the state’s only Easter parade, Snohomish Easter Parade includes floats, bands, kids, and pets on First Street in Snohomish, followed by an Easter bonnet contest.

item.title: Family music
item.description:
Saturday, April 4, 2026 at 11 AM

Location: https://www.google.com/maps/search/?api=1&query=Town%20Hall%20Seattle%2C%201119%208th%20Ave%2C%20Seattle%2C%20WA%2098101

First Hill (0.5 miles SE) Entertaining for both parents and children, The Brian Waite Band combines musical theater, imaginative storytelling, laughs, and original rock ‘n roll songs at Town Hall Seattle, 1119 8th Ave. in Seattle. Check out the free tickets for age 22 and younger.

Event details

item.title: Tea party at the longhouse
item.description:
Saturday, April 4, 2026 at 11 AM

Location: https://www.google.com/maps/search/?api=1&query=Duwamish%20Longhouse%2C%204705%20W%20Marginal%20Way%20SW%2C%20Seattle%2C%20WA%2098106

North Delridge (7.9 miles S) The $70 Princess Angeline Spring Tea usually features a speaker plus “tea and light refreshments” at the Duwamish Longhouse, 4705 W Marginal Way SW in Seattle. Princess Angeline was the eldest daughter of Chief Seattle. No children or pets.

Event details

item.title: Bunny bar crawl
item.description:
Saturday, April 4, 2026 at 12 PM

Location: Downtown Seattle

Wear bunny ears or a costume, and then hop from bar to bar during BunnyCon. Your ticket includes a wristband, live entertainment, and drink specials for age 21+.

Event details

item.title: Easter egg hunts
item.description:
Saturday, April 4, 2026 at 12 PM

Location: multiple locations

Kids Out & About Seattle’s Child Seattle Community Centers Tacoma (Pierce County) Olympia (Thurston County) Washington state by county

Event details

item.title: Daffodil parade
item.description:
Saturday, April 4, 2026 at 12 PM

Location: Pierce County

The Grand Floral Parade includes daffodil-covered floats, marching bands, clowns, and pirates that travel through Tacoma (10:15 a.m.), Puyallup (12:45 p.m.), Sumner (2:30 p.m.), and Orting (5 p.m.). It’s the highlight of the Daffodil Festival.

Event details

item.title: Everett Film Festival
item.description:
Saturday, April 4, 2026 at 12:30 PM

Location: https://www.google.com/maps/search/?api=1&query=Everett%20Performing%20Arts%20Center%2C%202710%20Wetmore%20Ave%2C%20Everett%2C%20WA%2098201

Everett (29 miles N) Everett Film Festival focuses on films by or about women at Everett Performing Arts Center, 2710 Wetmore Ave. in Everett.

Event details

item.title: Comedy showcase
item.description:
Saturday, April 4, 2026 at 7:30 PM

Location: https://www.google.com/maps/search/?api=1&query=Washington%20Hall%2C%20153%2014th%20Ave%2C%20Seattle%2C%20WA%2098122

Central District (1.5 miles SE) Hear seven of Seattle’s “most innovative comics” tell their best jokes at Undisputable Champions of Comedy at Washington Hall, 153 14th Ave. in Seattle for age 21+.

Event details

item.title: Easter restaurants
item.description:
Sunday, April 5, 2026 at 12 PM

Location: multiple locations

See recommended restaurants for Easter brunch or dinner. Gayot Guide — Seattle, Everett, Snoqualmie Open Table — Seattle, Eastside, Tacoma Seattle Met — Seattle, Kenmore, Renton Seattle’s Child — Seattle, Seatac, Eastside Yelp — Seattle, Eastside, Tacoma

Event details

item.title: Variety show
item.description:
Thursday, April 9, 2026 at 12 PM

Location: https://www.google.com/maps/search/?api=1&query=Broadway%20Performance%20Hall%2C%201625%20Broadway%2C%20Seattle%2C%20WA%2098122

Capitol Hill (0.8 miles NE) Billed as the world’s largest comedy/variety festival, Moisture Festival presents 30 shows, mostly at Broadway Performance Hall, 1625 Broadway in Seattle. Friday shows are for age 18+.

Event details

item.title: Taste of Rainier Valley
item.description:
Thursday, April 9, 2026 at 12 PM

Location: Rainier Valley (6 miles S)

Plate of Nations offers special deals on international meals. Click any of the 50 restaurants to see prices.

Event details

item.title: Hockey
item.description:
Thursday, April 9, 2026 at 12 PM

Location: https://www.google.com/maps/search/?api=1&query=Climate%20Pledge%20Arena%2C%20334%201st%20Ave%20N%2C%20Seattle%2C%20WA%2098109

Seattle Center (1.2 miles NW)

Event details

item.title: Student art show
item.description:
Thursday, April 9, 2026 at 12 PM

Location: https://www.google.com/maps/search/?api=1&query=9th%20Ave%20Gallery%2C%202014%209th%20Ave%2C%20Seattle%2C%20WA%2098121

Downtown (0.5 miles N) Cornish College of the Arts showcases the work of its Bachelor of Fine Arts students at the Art BFA Showcase at 9th Ave Gallery, 2014 9th Ave. in Seattle.

Event details

item.title: Art walks
item.description:
Thursday, April 9, 2026 at 12 PM

Location: multiple locations

Event details

item.title: Les Misérables
item.description:
Thursday, April 9, 2026 at 12 PM

Location: https://www.google.com/maps/search/?api=1&query=The%20Paramount%20Theatre%2C%20911%20Pine%20St%2C%20Seattle%2C%20WA%2098101

Downtown (0.3 miles NE) Les Misérables is the Broadway revival of the popular musical, which “tells an enthralling story of broken dreams and unrequited love, passion, sacrifice, and redemption” at The Paramount Theatre, 911 Pine St. in Seattle.

Event details

item.title: Music by candlelight
item.description:
Thursday, April 9, 2026 at 12 PM

Location: multiple locations

Event details

item.title: Tulip festival
item.description:
Thursday, April 9, 2026 at 12 PM

Location: https://www.google.com/maps/search/?api=1&query=Mount%20Vernon%20Downtown%20Association%2C%20223%20S%201st%20Street%2C%20Mount%20Vernon%2C%20WA%2098273

Mount Vernon (61 miles N) Check if flowers are blooming before you go. April 1 - 30 — Skagit Valley Tulip Festival features spectacular fields of blooming tulips and daffodils. Some fields are not free. April 11 (noon) — Tulip parade in La Conner. April 17 - 19 — Tulip Festival Street Fair is near Mount Vernon Downtown Association, 223 S 1st Street in Mount Vernon.

Event details

item.title: All-American musical
item.description:
Thursday, April 9, 2026 at 12 PM

Location: https://www.google.com/maps/search/?api=1&query=Village%20Theatre%2C%20303%20Front%20St%20N%2C%20Issaquah%2C%20WA%2098027

Issaquah (17 miles E) Take a rollicking trip back to the 1950s with the cast of Grease as they navigate the social waters of high school and love at Village Theatre, 303 Front St. N in Issaquah. Repeats later in Everett.

Event details

item.title: Live theater
item.description:
Thursday, April 9, 2026 at 12 PM

Event details

item.title: Free things to do
item.description:
Thursday, April 9, 2026 at 12 PM

Event details

item.title: Japanese festival
item.description:
Friday, April 10, 2026 at 12 PM

Location: https://www.google.com/maps/search/?api=1&query=Seattle%20Center%2C%20305%20Harrison%20St%2C%20Seattle%2C%20WA%2098109

Lower Queen Anne (0.8 miles NW) The Cherry Blossom & Japanese Cultural Festival celebrates Seattle’s friendship with Japan and Japan’s gift of 1000 cherry trees in 1976. Enjoy Japanese art, dance, music, martial arts, and food in the Armory and Fisher Pavilion at Seattle Center, 305 Harrison St. in Seattle.

Event details

item.title: Opera
item.description:
Friday, April 10, 2026 at 12 PM

Location: https://www.google.com/maps/search/?api=1&query=Meydenbauer%20Center%2C%2011000%20NE%206th%20St%2C%20Bellevue%2C%20WA%2098004

Bellevue (11 miles E) Madame Butterfly begins as a love story and becomes a tragedy when a young Japanese woman is abandoned by her American naval officer husband, at Meydenbauer Center, 11000 NE 6th St. in Bellevue. The music is played by Bellevue Symphony.

Event details

item.title: Radio controlled cars, etc.
item.description:
Friday, April 10, 2026 at 12 PM

Location: https://www.google.com/maps/search/?api=1&query=Evergreen%20State%20Fairgrounds%2C%2014405%20179th%20Ave%20SE%2C%20Monroe%2C%20WA%2098272

Monroe (33 miles NE) See vendors of radio controlled cars, airplanes, boats, robots, and trains at the $10 NW Model Hobby Expo at Evergreen State Fairgrounds, 14405 179th Ave. SE in Monroe. Try it yourself, and learn about clubs. Kids younger than age 10 are free. Parking free Fri. & Sun.

Event details

item.title: Ballet
item.description:
Friday, April 10, 2026 at 12 PM

Location: https://www.google.com/maps/search/?api=1&query=McCaw%20Hall%2C%20321%20Mercer%20St%2C%20Seattle%2C%20WA%2098109

Seattle Center (1.2 miles NW) Pacific Northwest Ballet performs the Romance-period ballet Giselle, an “ethereal, haunting, and tender masterpiece” about love beyond the grave, at McCaw Hall, 321 Mercer St. in Seattle. Synopsis.

Event details

item.title: Spring Fair
item.description:
Friday, April 10, 2026 at 12 PM

Location: https://www.google.com/maps/search/?api=1&query=Washington%20State%20Fair%20Events%20Center%2C%20110%209th%20Ave%20SW%2C%20Puyallup%2C%20WA%2098371

Puyallup (37 miles S) Spring Fair features farm animals, pig races, performing dogs, fair food, rides, entertainers, demolition derbies, and a kids’ zone at Washington State Fair Events Center, 110 9th Ave. SW in Puyallup. Open Friday, Saturday, and Sunday.

Event details

item.title: Baseball
item.description:
Friday, April 10, 2026 at 12 PM

Location: https://www.google.com/maps/search/?api=1&query=T-Mobile%20Park%2C%201250%201st%20Ave%20S%2C%20Seattle%2C%20WA%2098134

SoDo (1.6 miles S)

Event details

item.title: Taste of Seattle
item.description:
Friday, April 10, 2026 at 12 PM

Location: Seattle

Event details

item.title: Community Concert
item.description:
Friday, April 10, 2026 at 7:30 PM

Location: https://www.google.com/maps/search/?api=1&query=Benaroya%20Hall%2C%20200%20University%20St%2C%20Seattle%2C%20WA%2098101

Downtown (0.3 miles SW) The Seattle Symphony performs with members of the Seattle community during a Community Stages Fund: Fifth Anniversary Celebration at Benaroya Hall, 200 University St. in Seattle. [Please read their content warning about revealing costumes and gun violence.]

Event details

item.title: Classical concert
item.description:
Friday, April 10, 2026 at 8 PM

Location: https://www.google.com/maps/search/?api=1&query=Emerald%20City%20Music%2C%20415%20Westlake%20Ave%20N%2C%20Seattle%2C%20WA%2098109

South Lake Union (0.8 miles N) Originally written for piano, Canto Ostinato is a big hit in the Netherlands, performed by six artists at Emerald City Music, 415 Westlake Ave. N in Seattle. Repeats on April 11 in Olympia.

Event details

item.title: Toy show
item.description:
Saturday, April 11, 2026 at 9 AM

Location: https://www.google.com/maps/search/?api=1&query=Angel%20of%20the%20Winds%20Arena%2C%202000%20Hewitt%20Ave%2C%20Everett%2C%20WA%2098201

Everett (28 miles N) Shop from 250 vendors of vintage and modern toys, comics, and collectibles at the Washington State Toy Show at Angel of the Winds Arena, 2000 Hewitt Ave. in Everett. The first hour costs more. Children age 12 and younger are free.

Event details

item.title: Small tea festival
item.description:
Saturday, April 11, 2026 at 11 AM

Location: https://www.google.com/maps/search/?api=1&query=Floral%20Hall%2C%20802%20E%20Mukilteo%20Blvd%2C%20Everett%2C%20WA%2098203

Everett (29 miles N) Cascadia Spring Tea Festival features local vendors, tea tasting (bring your own cup), tea arts and crafts, and workshops at Floral Hall, 802 E Mukilteo Blvd. in Everett.

Event details

item.title: Arts & crafts show
item.description:
Saturday, April 11, 2026 at 12 PM

Location: https://www.google.com/maps/search/?api=1&query=Hangar%2030%20at%20Magnuson%20Park%2C%206310%20NE%2074th%20St%2C%20Seattle%2C%20WA%2098115

Sand Point (7.6 miles NE) Best of the Northwest Spring show has 100 artists displaying jewelry, clothing, paintings, glass art, and sculptures plus food trucks in Hangar 30 at Magnuson Park, 6310 NE 74th St. in Seattle. Children age 12 and younger are free. No pets. Repeats in Nov.

Event details

item.title: Whale festival on Whidbey Island
item.description:
Saturday, April 11, 2026 at 12 PM

Location: https://www.google.com/maps/search/?api=1&query=Langley%20Whale%20Center%2C%20105%20Anthes%20Ave%2C%20Langley%2C%20WA%2098260

Langley (35 miles N via ferry) Welcome the Whales honors Orca whales that return every spring with costume making (Sat. 11 a.m.), a parade & beach ceremony (Sat. 2 p.m.), a $16 gray whale documentary (Sun. 11 a.m.), and a two-hour boat trip for $120 (Sun. 3 to 5 p.m.).

Event details

item.title: Dining cruise
item.description:
Saturday, April 11, 2026 at 12 PM

Location: https://www.google.com/maps/search/?api=1&query=Waterways%20Cruises%2C%20901%20Fairview%20Ave%20N%2C%20Seattle%2C%20WA%2098109

Eastlake (1.3 miles N) Enjoy brunch or dinner at your own private table, with a beverage upon boarding and a two-hour guided cruise on Lake Union & Lake Washington. Departs from Waterways Cruises, 901 Fairview Ave. N in Seattle. April 11 to 26 — Brunch (11 a.m.) all ages April 17 & 24 — Dinner (6 p.m.) ages 13+

Event details

item.title: Central Asian festival
item.description:
Saturday, April 11, 2026 at 1 PM

Location: https://www.google.com/maps/search/?api=1&query=Shoreline%20Community%20College%2C%2016101%20Greenwood%20Ave%20N%2C%20Shoreline%2C%20WA%2098133

Shoreline (11 miles NW) Your ticket to Navruz Celebration includes a Central Asian meal, dance performances, a fashion show, and a children’s area in the 9000 Building at Shoreline Community College, 16101 Greenwood Ave. N in Shoreline.

Event details

item.title: Wine walk
item.description:
Saturday, April 11, 2026 at 1 PM

Location: https://www.google.com/maps/search/?api=1&query=Discover%20Burien%2C%20611%20SW%20152nd%20St%2C%20Burien%2C%20WA%2098166

Burien (12 miles S) Visit Burien shops that host wine tasting with a $53 ticket to Burien Uncorked for age 21+. Begin at Discover Burien, 611 SW 152nd St. in Burien. Held in April and August.

Event details

item.title: Farm dinner
item.description:
Saturday, April 11, 2026 at 5 PM

Location: https://www.google.com/maps/search/?api=1&query=Carnation%20Farms%2C%2028907%20NE%20Carnation%20Farm%20Rd%2C%20Carnation%2C%20WA%2098014

Carnation (27 miles E) A $263 ticket to Farmview Dinner includes a welcome cocktail plus “three expertly crafted farm-to-table courses with curated wine pairings” at Carnation Farms, 28901 NE Carnation Farm Road in Carnation. FAQ.

Event details

item.title: Outer-space party
item.description:
Saturday, April 11, 2026 at 6 PM

Location: https://www.google.com/maps/search/?api=1&query=The%20Museum%20of%20Flight%2C%209404%20E%20Marginal%20Way%20S%2C%20Seattle%2C%20WA%2098108

Georgetown (8 miles S) Celebrate the first man to orbit the earth in 1961 at $65 Yuri’s Night, a space-themed spectacle that transforms the Museum of Flight into an intergalactic nightclub for age 21+ with electronic DJ music, lighted art, drinks, and food trucks at 9404 E Marginal Way S in Seattle. Costumes are welcome.

Event details

item.title: Opera
item.description:
Sunday, April 12, 2026 at 12 PM

Location: https://www.google.com/maps/search/?api=1&query=Meydenbauer%20Center%2C%2011000%20NE%206th%20St%2C%20Bellevue%2C%20WA%2098004

Bellevue (11 miles E) Madame Butterfly begins as a love story and becomes a tragedy when a young Japanese woman is abandoned by her American naval officer husband, at Meydenbauer Center, 11000 NE 6th St. in Bellevue. The music is played by Bellevue Symphony.

Event details

item.title: Tasting event
item.description:
Sunday, April 12, 2026 at 4 PM

Location: https://www.google.com/maps/search/?api=1&query=Block%2041%2C%20115%20Bell%20St%2C%20Seattle%2C%20WA%2098121

Belltown (0.5 miles NW) The Big Taste includes 10 tastes of wine, beer, cider, or spirits, with live music and food for sale at Block 41, 115 Bell St. in Seattle. Bring age 21+ ID. Tickets are $60.

Event details

item.title: Jazz
item.description:
Sunday, April 12, 2026 at 6 PM

Location: https://www.google.com/maps/search/?api=1&query=Seattle%20First%20Baptist%2C%201111%20Harvard%20Ave%2C%20Seattle%2C%20WA%2098122

First Hill (0.9 miles E) Seattle Jazz Vespers features the five members of Deseo Carmin playing “a fiery fusion of Latin rhythms, soulful jazz/funk grooves, and the passionate spirit of flamenco” at Seattle First Baptist, 1111 Harvard Ave. in Seattle. Parking.

item.title: Art party
item.description:
Tuesday, April 14, 2026 at 6 PM

Location: https://www.google.com/maps/search/?api=1&query=Lynnwood%20Event%20Center%2C%203711%20196th%20St%20SW%2C%20Lynnwood%2C%20WA%2098036

Lynnwood (17 miles NE) Meet dozens of Pacific Northwest artists, see their bird-themed art, eat complimentary appetizers, buy drinks at the bar, and hear live music during Meet the Artists Reception at Lynnwood Event Center, 3711 196th St. SW in Lynnwood. The exhibition is open through June 22, 2026.

Event details

item.title: Dancing with the Stars
item.description:
Wednesday, April 15, 2026 at 7:30 PM

Location: https://www.google.com/maps/search/?api=1&query=WaMu%20Theater%2C%20800%20Occidental%20Ave%20S%2C%20Seattle%2C%20WA%2098134

Pioneer Square (1.5 miles S) Dancing with the Stars Live features the TV show’s fan favorites and professional dancers performing dances that range from ballroom and jazz to modern and hip-hop at WaMu Theater, 800 Occidental Ave. S in Seattle.

Event details

item.title: Tacoma museum night
item.description:
Thursday, April 16, 2026 at 12 PM

Location: Tacoma

Event details

item.title: Courageous films
item.description:
Thursday, April 16, 2026 at 12 PM

Location: https://www.google.com/maps/search/?api=1&query=Pacific%20Tower%2C%201200%2012th%20Ave%20S%2C%20Seattle%2C%20WA%2098144

Beacon Hill (2.1 miles SE) Social Justice Film Festival showcases “fresh voices and brave storytelling that demonstrate courage in action” by resilient people around the world at Pacific Tower, 1200 12th Ave. S in Seattle. Some films are followed by discussions.

Event details

item.title: 1980s sing along
item.description:
Thursday, April 16, 2026 at 8 PM

Location: https://www.google.com/maps/search/?api=1&query=Central%20Cinema%2C%201411%2021st%20Ave%2C%20Seattle%2C%20WA%2098122

Central District (1.9 miles E) The Totally ‘80s Sing Along projects 30 music videos with subtitles on screen so you can sing along to Madonna, Prince, Michael Jackson, Billy Idol, George Michael, Depeche Mode, Whitney Houston, and David Bowie at Central Cinema, 1411 21st Ave. in Seattle. To buy a $17 ticket, click “8:00” on their page, and then “Continue As Guest.”

Event details

item.title: Clam festival
item.description:
Friday, April 17, 2026 at 12 PM

Location: https://www.google.com/maps/search/?api=1&query=Veteran's%20Field%2C%20111%203rd%20St%20SE%2C%20Long%20Beach%2C%20WA%2098631

Long Beach (172 miles SW) Long Beach Razor Clam Festival has seafood, a beer & wine garden, vendors, live music, and clam digging lessons, mostly at Veteran's Field, 111 3rd St. SE in Long Beach.

Event details

item.title: Plant show
item.description:
Friday, April 17, 2026 at 12 PM

Location: https://www.google.com/maps/search/?api=1&query=Lynnwood%20Event%20Center%2C%203711%20196th%20St%20SW%2C%20Lynnwood%2C%20WA%2098036

Lynnwood (17 miles NE) Admire and shop for a variety of house plants (begonia, orchid, cactus, succulent, carnivorous, and African violet) and attend classes at the Spring Indoor Plant Festival at Lynnwood Event Center, 3711 196th St. SW in Lynnwood. Children younger than age 15 are free. Friday is a $50 evening party.

Event details

item.title: Poetry festival
item.description:
Friday, April 17, 2026 at 12 PM

Location: multiple locations

Cadence: Video Poetry Festival is “a series of screenings, workshops, and discussions on the genre of video poetry” with works from multiple countries at two venues. Schedule. April 17 - 19 — Northwest Film Forum April 30 — Frye Art Museum

Event details

item.title: Family square dance
item.description:
Friday, April 17, 2026 at 7:30 PM

Location: https://www.google.com/maps/search/?api=1&query=Urban%20Family%20Brewing%2C%201103%20NW%2052nd%20St%2C%20Seattle%2C%20WA%2098107

Ballard (5 miles NW) The $10 Square Dance has a live band and a caller to instruct beginners at Urban Family Brewing, 1103 NW 52nd St. in Seattle. Children younger than age 18 are free.

Event details

item.title: Hawaiian festival
item.description:
Saturday, April 18, 2026 at 11 AM

Location: https://www.google.com/maps/search/?api=1&query=Tacoma%20Community%20College%2C%206501%20S%2019th%20St%2C%20Tacoma%2C%20WA%2098466

Tacoma (38 miles SW) Aha Mele Hawaiian Festival has music, hula, vendors, food, workshops, and kids’ activities at Tacoma Community College, 6501 S 19th St. in Tacoma.

Event details

item.title: Children’s festival
item.description:
Saturday, April 18, 2026 at 11 AM

Location: https://www.google.com/maps/search/?api=1&query=Fisher%20Pavilion%2C%20305%20Harrison%20St%2C%20Seattle%2C%20WA%2098109

Seattle Center (1 mile NW) Children from around the world dance, sing, play music, make origami, and get faces painted at the International Children’s Friendship Festival in Fisher Pavilion at Seattle Center, 305 Harrison St. in Seattle. See the schedule of performances.

Event details

item.title: Run
item.description:
Saturday, April 18, 2026 at 12 PM

Location: https://www.google.com/maps/search/?api=1&query=Seward%20Park%2C%205900%20Lake%20Washington%20Blvd%20S%2C%20Seattle%2C%20WA%2098118

Seward Park (6 miles SE) Seattle Cherry Blossom is a run or walk under the cherry trees at Seward Park, 5900 Lake Washington Blvd. S in Seattle. Saturday — 5K (sold out) & 10K Sunday — half marathon

Event details

item.title: Roller derby
item.description:
Saturday, April 18, 2026 at 12 PM

Location: https://www.google.com/maps/search/?api=1&query=Hangar%2030%20at%20Magnuson%20Park%2C%206310%20NE%2074th%20St%2C%20Seattle%2C%20WA%2098115

Sand Point (7.6 miles NE) Watch Rat City Roller Derby play exciting games at 3:30 & 6 p.m., with snacks for sale and a beer garden at Hangar 30 in Magnuson Park, 6310 NE 74th St. in Seattle. Doors open 2 p.m.

Event details

item.title: Crab races and crab feed
item.description:
Saturday, April 18, 2026 at 12 PM

Location: https://www.google.com/maps/search/?api=1&query=Westport%20Convention%20Center%2C%20421%20Neddie%20Rose%20Dr%2C%20Westport%2C%20WA%2098595

Westport (132 miles SW) Watch crabs race and eat crab at the World Class Crab Races and Crab Feed inside a tent in the parking lot at Westport Convention Center, 421 Neddie Rose Drive in Westport. There is also a crab catching derby with prizes.

Event details

item.title: New ideas conference
item.description:
Saturday, April 18, 2026 at 12 PM

Location: https://www.google.com/maps/search/?api=1&query=Town%20Hall%20Seattle%2C%201119%208th%20Ave%2C%20Seattle%2C%20WA%2098101

First Hill (0.5 miles SE) “Explore art, philosophy, and technology as tools for resistance and optimism” during Night of Ideas, a program that “examines the legacy of the Enlightenment against modern challenges like the climate crisis and the erosion of democratic values.” Sat. (5 p.m.) — Keynote address, visual art, and panel discussion at Town Hall Seattle. Sun. (2 p.m.) — Hands-on art workshops and discussions at Gage Academy of Art.

Event details

item.title: Cat convention
item.description:
Saturday, April 18, 2026 at 12 PM

Location: https://www.google.com/maps/search/?api=1&query=Seattle%20Center%20Exhibition%20Hall%2C%20301%20Mercer%20St%2C%20Seattle%2C%20WA%2098109

Seattle Center (1.2 miles NW) Pop Cats celebrates cats and pop culture with artwork, adoptable cats, speakers, workshops, and vendors at Seattle Center Exhibition Hall, 301 Mercer St. in Seattle. Children younger than age 4 are free. You can bring your cat.

Event details

item.title: Weird arts & crafts show
item.description:
Saturday, April 18, 2026 at 12 PM

Location: https://www.google.com/maps/search/?api=1&query=Evergreen%20State%20Fairgrounds%2C%2014405%20179th%20Ave%20SE%2C%20Monroe%2C%20WA%2098272

Monroe (33 miles NE) See unusual creations by 100 artists and crafters at Oddmall Emporium of the Weird, and visit the food trucks at Evergreen State Fairgrounds, 14405 179th Ave. SE in Monroe. Free admission and parking. Held in March and November.

Event details

item.title: Earth Day
item.description:
Saturday, April 18, 2026 at 12 PM

Location: multiple locations

Event details

item.title: Soccer
item.description:
Saturday, April 18, 2026 at 12 PM

Location: https://www.google.com/maps/search/?api=1&query=Lumen%20Field%2C%20800%20Occidental%20Ave%20S%2C%20Seattle%2C%20WA%2098134

Pioneer Square (1.3 miles S)

Event details

item.title: Distillery tasting
item.description:
Saturday, April 18, 2026 at 2 PM

Location: https://www.google.com/maps/search/?api=1&query=Thomas%20Family%20Farm%2C%209010%20Marsh%20Rd%2C%20Snohomish%2C%20WA%2098296

Snohomish (28 miles NE) Meet local distillers, sample eight of their products, buy food, hear music, and order cocktails at $42 Snohomish on the Rocks at Thomas Family Farm, 9010 Marsh Road in Snohomish. Bring age 21+ ID.

Event details

item.title: Dinner dance
item.description:
Saturday, April 18, 2026 at 6 PM

Location: https://www.google.com/maps/search/?api=1&query=Polish%20Cultural%20Center%2C%201714%2018th%20Ave%2C%20Seattle%2C%20WA%2098122

Capitol Hill (1.8 miles NE) Lure of the Deep: Fantasy Ball features dinner, a performance by Syrena Seattle, swing and bachata dance classes, a DJ, an orchestra, and a no-host bar at Polish Cultural Center, 1714 18th Ave. in Seattle for age 21+. Tickets are $85, or $55 for younger than age 25.

item.title: Night market
item.description:
Saturday, April 18, 2026 at 6 PM

Location: https://www.google.com/maps/search/?api=1&query=Columbia%20City%20Night%20Market%2C%204850%2037th%20Ave%20S%2C%20Seattle%2C%20WA%2098118

Columbia City (5.5 miles SE) Monthly Columbia City Night Market has 8 food trucks, 20 makers and vendors, live music, and a beer garden on 37th Avenue S. (from S. Ferdinand to S. Edmunds). Walk 10 minutes from Columbia City rail station to 4850 37th Ave. S in Seattle.

Event details

item.title: Short films in Shoreline
item.description:
Saturday, April 18, 2026 at 6 PM

Location: https://www.google.com/maps/search/?api=1&query=Shoreline%20Community%20College%2C%2016101%20Greenwood%20Ave%20N%2C%20Shoreline%2C%20WA%2098133

Shoreline (11 miles NW) Shoreline Short Short Film Festival screens a dozen films that are 3 to 13 minutes long and made by local emerging and developing filmmakers, with concessions for sale at Shoreline Community College, 16101 Greenwood Ave. N in Shoreline. Vote for your favorite film to determine who gets a golden Sasquatch statue.

Event details

item.title: Inspirational talks
item.description:
Sunday, April 19, 2026 at 10:30 AM

Location: https://www.google.com/maps/search/?api=1&query=Kane%20Hall%2C%204069%20Spokane%20Ln%2C%20Seattle%2C%20WA%2098105

University District (4.9 miles NE) Hear short talks by passionate speakers during $20 TEDxUofW at the University of Washington in Kane Hall, 4069 Spokane Lane in Seattle. This year’s theme is about memories: “imprints we leave on the world and those the world leaves on us.”

Event details

item.title: Daffodil boat parade
item.description:
Sunday, April 19, 2026 at 11:30 AM

Location: https://www.google.com/maps/search/?api=1&query=Tacoma%20Yacht%20Club%2C%205401%20Yacht%20Club%20Rd%2C%20Tacoma%2C%20WA%2098407

Tacoma (39 miles S) Watch decorated boats in the Daffodil Marine Festival and Parade, which departs from Tacoma Yacht Club, 5401 Yacht Club Road in Tacoma. The boats go southeast along Ruston Way and then into Thea Foss Waterway near downtown Tacoma before turning around and returning by 2:30 p.m.

Event details

item.title: Restaurant week
item.description:
Sunday, April 19, 2026 at 12 PM

Location: Seattle & Eastside

Event details

item.title: Party for dance fans
item.description:
Sunday, April 19, 2026 at 12:30 PM

Location: https://www.google.com/maps/search/?api=1&query=Central%20Cinema%2C%201411%2021st%20Ave%2C%20Seattle%2C%20WA%2098122

Central District (1.9 miles E) See a live dance performance, watch a video of another performance, network, and enjoy Korean–Southern fusion refreshments during O-Jak Watch Party at Central Cinema, 1411 21st Ave. in Seattle.

Event details

item.title: Classical music
item.description:
Sunday, April 19, 2026 at 3 PM

Location: https://www.google.com/maps/search/?api=1&query=Washington%20Hall%2C%20153%2014th%20Ave%2C%20Seattle%2C%20WA%2098122

Central District (1.5 miles SE) Hear French baroque music performed by four musicians in the program Temple de la Gloire at Washington Hall, 153 14th Ave. in Seattle.

Event details

item.title: State park day
item.description:
Wednesday, April 22, 2026 at 12 PM

Location: All state parks

Event details

item.title: Glass-school tours
item.description:
Wednesday, April 22, 2026 at 12 PM

Location: https://www.google.com/maps/search/?api=1&query=Pilchuk%20Glass%20School%2C%201201%20316th%20St%20NW%2C%20Stanwood%2C%20WA%2098292

Stanwood (54 miles N) Watch hot glass demonstrations, meet the artists, take a tour of the campus founded by Dale Chihuly, and shop for glass art during Spring Tours at Pilchuk Glass School, 1201 316th St. NW in Stanwood. [Click all of the ticket buttons to see all available timeslots.]

Event details

item.title: Healthcare
item.description:
Thursday, April 23, 2026 at 12 PM

Location: https://www.google.com/maps/search/?api=1&query=Fisher%20Pavilion%2C%20305%20Harrison%20St%2C%20Seattle%2C%20WA%2098109

Seattle Center (1 mile NW) Seattle/King County Clinic gives dental, vision, and medical care to anyone (no identification necessary) at Seattle Center. The first tickets are distributed at 5:30 a.m. in Fisher Pavilion, 305 Harrison St. in Seattle. Free parking in Mercer Street Garage, 650 3rd Ave North.

Event details

item.title: Apple Blossom Festival
item.description:
Thursday, April 23, 2026 at 12 PM

Location: https://www.google.com/maps/search/?api=1&query=Memorial%20Park%2C%202%20S%20Chelan%20Ave%2C%20Wenatchee%2C%20WA%2098801

Wenatchee (148 miles E) Washington State Apple Blossom Festival in Eastern Washington has entertainment, food vendors, a beer garden, carnival rides, an arts & crafts fair, a car parade (Fri. 6 p.m.), a car show (Sat.), and a grand parade (Sat. 11 a.m.) in and around Memorial Park, 2 S. Chelan Ave. in Wenatchee. Schedule.

Event details

item.title: Pub night for charity
item.description:
Thursday, April 23, 2026 at 5 PM

Location: https://www.google.com/maps/search/?api=1&query=Rough%20%26%20Tumble%20Pub%2C%205309%2022nd%20Ave%20NW%2C%20Seattle%2C%20WA%2098107

Ballard (5.2 miles NW) Brew Review is a $30 fundraiser for Unemployment Law Project offering beer, cider, wine, games, and music at Rough & Tumble Pub, 5309 22nd Ave. NW in Seattle. Your ticket includes some food and two drinks. Free for children age 12 and younger.

Event details

item.title: Rodeo
item.description:
Friday, April 24, 2026 at 12 PM

Location: https://www.google.com/maps/search/?api=1&query=Tacoma%20Dome%2C%202727%20E%20D%20Street%2C%20Tacoma%2C%20WA%2098421

Tacoma (33 miles S) Professional Bull Riders compete to see who can last the longest atop a bucking bull at the Tacoma Dome, 2727 East D Street in Tacoma.

Event details

item.title: Olympia arts weekend
item.description:
Friday, April 24, 2026 at 12 PM

Location: https://www.google.com/maps/search/?api=1&query=Sylvester%20Park%2C%20615%20Washington%20St%20SE%2C%20Olympia%2C%20WA%2098501

Olympia (62 miles S) Admire art Friday evening and Saturday at Olympia Arts Walk. Join a luminary procession (Fri. 8:30 p.m.). Watch people parade in animal costumes in the Procession of the Species (Sat. 4:30 p.m.). Both processions go past Sylvester Park, 615 Washington St. SE in Olympia. Street parking is free after 5 p.m. and on weekends.

Event details

item.title: Body-building expo
item.description:
Friday, April 24, 2026 at 12 PM

Location: https://www.google.com/maps/search/?api=1&query=Meydenbauer%20Center%2C%2011000%20NE%206th%20St%2C%20Bellevue%2C%20WA%2098004

Bellevue (11 miles E) Emerald Cup is a bodybuilding, fitness, figure, bikini, and physique championship with health-industry vendors at Meydenbauer Center, 11000 NE 6th St. in Bellevue.

Event details

item.title: Home show
item.description:
Friday, April 24, 2026 at 12 PM

Location: https://www.google.com/maps/search/?api=1&query=Evergreen%20State%20Fairgrounds%2C%2014405%20179th%20Ave%20SE%2C%20Monroe%2C%20WA%2098272

Monroe (33 miles NE) Evergreen Home Show promises 200 booths of “specialized local businesses ready to help you customize, update, or even design, the home you have always wanted” at Evergreen State Fairgrounds, 14405 179th Ave. SE in Monroe. Held in spring and fall.

Event details

item.title: Victorian festival
item.description:
Friday, April 24, 2026 at 12 PM

Location: https://www.google.com/maps/search/?api=1&query=Cotton%20Building%2C%20607%20Water%20St%2C%20Port%20Townsend%2C%20WA%2098368

Port Townsend (58 miles NW via ferry) Victorian Heritage Festival has walking tours, teas, opening houses, a Saturday Victorian fashion show, and a contra dance. Festival headquarters is in the Cotton Building, 607 Water St. in Port Townsend.

Event details

item.title: Rugby
item.description:
Friday, April 24, 2026 at 12 PM

Location: https://www.google.com/maps/search/?api=1&query=Starfire%20Sports%2C%2014800%20Starfire%20Way%2C%20Tukwila%2C%20WA%2098188

Tukwila (12 miles S)

Event details

item.title: Dance party
item.description:
Friday, April 24, 2026 at 5:30 PM

Location: https://www.google.com/maps/search/?api=1&query=The%20Swedish%20Club%2C%201920%20Dexter%20Ave%20N%2C%20Seattle%2C%20WA%2098109

Westlake (1.9 miles N) The $72 Abba Night begins with a DJ playing Abba and Euro pop music, followed by Seattle tribute band Abbagrahics performing live on the dance floor, with Nordic food for sale at The Swedish Club, 1920 Dexter Ave. N in Seattle for age 21+.

Event details

item.title: Eastside wine walk
item.description:
Friday, April 24, 2026 at 6 PM

Location: https://www.google.com/maps/search/?api=1&query=Downtown%20Issaquah%20Association%2C%20232%20Front%20St%20N%2C%20Issaquah%2C%20WA%2098027

Issaquah (17 miles E) The $39 Downtown Issaquah Wine & Art Walk includes 12 tasting tokens to redeem at shops along picturesque Front Street. VIP at Capri Cellars. General admission at Downtown Issaquah Association, 232 Front St. N in Issaquah for age 21+. Bring your own glass. Held several times a year.

Event details

item.title: Dance: Alice in Wonderland
item.description:
Friday, April 24, 2026 at 7:30 PM

Location: https://www.google.com/maps/search/?api=1&query=Meany%20Center%20for%20the%20Performing%20Arts%2C%204040%20George%20Washington%20Lane%20NE%2C%20Seattle%2C%20WA%2098195

University District (4.6 miles NE) The dance company MOMIX “creates surreal and fantastic worlds with light, shadow, props and the human body” in Alice at Meany Center for the Performing Arts, 4040 George Washington Lane NE in Seattle.

Event details

item.title: Architectural tour
item.description:
Saturday, April 25, 2026 at 10 AM

Location: Seattle area

Join Seattle Architecture Foundation on a $35 walking tour. No pets. FAQ. Mount Baker neighborhood tour

Event details

item.title: Clean energy fair
item.description:
Saturday, April 25, 2026 at 10 AM

Location: https://www.google.com/maps/search/?api=1&query=PUD%20Auditorium%20Theater%2C%202320%20California%20St%2C%20Everett%2C%20WA%2098201

Everett (29 miles N) See electric cars, touch a utility truck, watch a high-voltage electricity show, find out about solar energy, and shop at food trucks during Energy Block Party at PUD Auditorium Theater, 2320 California St. in Everett.

Event details

item.title: Tacoma kids’ parade
item.description:
Saturday, April 25, 2026 at 10 AM

Location: https://www.google.com/maps/search/?api=1&query=N%20Proctor%20St%20%26%20N%2028th%20St%2C%20Tacoma%2C%20WA%2098407

Tacoma (37 miles S) Billed as the largest children’s parade in the state, the Junior Daffodil Parade is four blocks long, beginning at N Proctor St. & N 28th St. in Tacoma.

Event details

item.title: Green home tour
item.description:
Saturday, April 25, 2026 at 11 AM

Location: https://www.google.com/maps/d/viewer?mid=1GqeqbOZpeo3NG5bfahQSgoDqdGJY9Pw&usp=sharing

multiple locations Register online for the self-guided Northwest Green Home Tour to see seven solar-powered, energy-efficient, and sustainably built homes. An additional three homes in remote areas are available on Sunday.

Event details

item.title: Spirits tasting
item.description:
Saturday, April 25, 2026 at 12 PM

Location: https://www.google.com/maps/search/?api=1&query=Pacific%20Place%2C%20600%20Pine%20St%2C%20Seattle%2C%20WA%2098101

Downtown (0.2 miles E) The highlight of Seattle Cocktail Week is the $69 Carnival of Cocktails, where adults can watch cocktail demonstrations, shop at food trucks, and use 12 tokens to taste spirits or mini cocktails at Pacific Place, 600 Pine St. in Seattle.

Event details

item.title: Magnolia wine walk
item.description:
Saturday, April 25, 2026 at 12 PM

Location: https://www.google.com/maps/search/?api=1&query=Modeles%20Home%20Furnishings%2C%203220%20W%20McGraw%20St%2C%20Seattle%2C%20WA%2098199

Magnolia (3.9 miles NW) Visit local shops to taste wine from a dozen wineries during Spring Vino in the Village. Start by picking up your wine glass at Modeles Home Furnishings, 3220 W McGraw St. in Seattle. Also held in September.

Event details

item.title: French concert
item.description:
Saturday, April 25, 2026 at 12 PM

Location: multiple locations

Event details

item.title: Student acrobats
item.description:
Saturday, April 25, 2026 at 12 PM

Location: https://www.google.com/maps/search/?api=1&query=Emerald%20City%20Trapeze%20Arts%2C%202702%206th%20Ave%20S%2C%20Seattle%2C%20WA%2098134

SoDo (2.8 miles S) Watch teenagers from the School of Acrobatics and New Circus Arts act out a creative circus story as they unicycle, juggle, and fly through the air during Circus Gone Wrong: The Case of the Missing Props at Emerald City Trapeze Arts, 2702 6th Ave. S in Seattle.

Event details

item.title: Jazz walk in North Bend
item.description:
Saturday, April 25, 2026 at 4 PM

Location: https://www.google.com/maps/search/?api=1&query=North%20Bend%20Theatre%2C%20125%20Bendigo%20Blvd%2C%20North%20Bend%2C%20WA%2098045

North Bend (30 miles E) Head to the mountains to hear live jazz during North Bend Jazz Walk in 18 venues centered around North Bend Theatre, 125 Bendigo Blvd. in North Bend. A few venues are for age 21+. The first two hours are high school jazz bands.

Event details

item.title: Underground art show
item.description:
Saturday, April 25, 2026 at 8 PM

Location: https://www.google.com/maps/search/?api=1&query=El%20Corazon%2C%20109%20Eastlake%20Ave%20E%2C%20Seattle%2C%20WA%2098109

South Lake Union (0.6 miles NE) The $21 Pancakes & Booze Art Show is a traveling exhibition by 70 artists, with pancakes included, DJ music, live body painting, and a bar at El Corazon, 109 Eastlake Ave. E in Seattle for age 21+.

Event details

item.title: Masquerade ball
item.description:
Saturday, April 25, 2026 at 8 PM

Location: https://www.google.com/maps/search/?api=1&query=Nippon%20Kan%20Theatre%20%2C%20628%20S%20Washington%20St%2C%20Seattle%2C%20WA%2098104

International District (1 mile S) Dance the night away at $105 Venice is Sinking Masquerade Ball, which has a Venetian theme, acrobats, psychics, and a magician at Nippon Kan Theatre , 628 S Washington St. in Seattle for age 21+. A decorative mask is required.

Event details

item.title: Touch a Truck
item.description:
Sunday, April 26, 2026 at 9 AM

Location: https://www.google.com/maps/search/?api=1&query=Parking%20Lot%20E1%2C%20Montlake%20Blvd%20NE%2C%20Seattle%2C%20WA%2098105

University District (4.4 miles NE) Children can touch and explore heavy machinery and emergency vehicles, talk to the drivers, and play games at Touch a Truck in Parking Lot E1, just south of University of Washington Golf Range off Montlake Blvd. NE in Seattle. Paid parking in E18. Children younger than age 2 are free. Food trucks available. No pets.

Event details

item.title: Dinosaur day
item.description:
Sunday, April 26, 2026 at 10 AM

Location: https://www.google.com/maps/search/?api=1&query=The%20Burke%20Museum%2C%204300%2015th%20Ave%20NE%2C%20Seattle%2C%20WA%2098105

University District (4.5 miles NE) Dig up fossils, see exhibits, and talk to paleontologists about dinosaurs during DinoFest at The Burke Museum, 4300 15th Ave. NE in Seattle. Buy tickets online to reserve an entry time.

Event details

item.title: Run around windmills
item.description:
Sunday, April 26, 2026 at 12 PM

Location: https://www.google.com/maps/search/?api=1&query=Wild%20Horse%20Wind%20and%20Solar%20Facility%2C%2025901%20Vantage%20Hwy%2C%20Ellensburg%2C%20WA%2098926

Ellensburg (128 miles E) Run Like the Wind Running Festival includes running events (5K, 10K, 10 mile, kid’s dash), finish-line food, beer samples, a wildflower walk, and a windmill tour at Wild Horse Wind and Solar Facility, 25901 Vantage Hwy in Ellensburg.

Event details

item.title: World explorer show
item.description:
Sunday, April 26, 2026 at 12 PM

Location: https://www.google.com/maps/search/?api=1&query=Benaroya%20Hall%2C%20200%20University%20St%2C%20Seattle%2C%20WA%2098101

Downtown (0.3 miles SW) National Geographic Live presents scientists, photographers, filmmakers, and adventurers live on stage with amazing photos and video at Benaroya Hall, 200 University St. in Seattle. April is about urban wildlife.

Event details

item.title: Soccer
item.description:
Sunday, April 26, 2026 at 12 PM

Location: https://www.google.com/maps/search/?api=1&query=Lumen%20Field%2C%20800%20Occidental%20Ave%20S%2C%20Seattle%2C%20WA%2098134

Pioneer Square (1.3 miles S)

Event details

item.title: Chowder fundraiser
item.description:
Sunday, April 26, 2026 at 4 PM

Location: https://www.google.com/maps/search/?api=1&query=Foss%20Waterway%20Seaport%20Museum%2C%20705%20Dock%20St%2C%20Tacoma%2C%20WA%2098402

Tacoma (35 miles S) A $100 ticket to Chowder Social includes “a hosted social hour, dinner with a variety of chowders and salads, silent and live auctions, and a dessert dash” at Foss Waterway Seaport Museum, 705 Dock St. in Tacoma for age 21+.

Event details

item.title: Peruvian fusion band
item.description:
Tuesday, April 28, 2026 at 7:30 PM

Location: https://www.google.com/maps/search/?api=1&query=Town%20Hall%20Seattle%2C%201119%208th%20Ave%2C%20Seattle%2C%20WA%2098101

First Hill (0.5 miles SE) Global Rhythms features Novalima, a group of four friends from Peru, playing Afro-Peruvian music at Town Hall Seattle, 1119 8th Ave. in Seattle. See the free tickets for age 22 and younger.

Event details

item.title: Black film festival
item.description:
Thursday, April 30, 2026 at 12 PM

Location: https://www.google.com/maps/search/?api=1&query=Langston%20Hughes%20Performing%20Arts%20Institute%2C%20104%2017th%20Ave%20S%2C%20Seattle%2C%20WA%2098144

Central District (1.7 miles SE) Seattle Black Film Festival “features film screenings, hands-on workshops, panel discussions, and in-depth chats with filmmakers” at Langston Hughes Performing Arts Institute, 104 17th Ave. S, Seattle.

Event details

item.title: RV show
item.description:
Thursday, April 30, 2026 at 12 PM

Location: https://www.google.com/maps/search/?api=1&query=Washington%20State%20Fair%20Events%20Center%2C%20110%209th%20Ave%20SW%2C%20Puyallup%2C%20WA%2098371

Puyallup (37 miles S) See the latest recreational vehicles at Puyallup RV Show at Washington State Fair Events Center, 110 9th Ave. SW in Puyallup. Your children age 17 and younger are free. Wear sunscreen.

Event details

item.title: Eastside wine walk
item.description:
Thursday, April 30, 2026 at 5 PM

Location: https://www.google.com/maps/search/?api=1&query=Cinemark%2C%2012600%20120th%20Ave%20NE%2C%20Kirkland%2C%20WA%2098034

Kirkland (15 miles NE) A $51 ticket to the Wine Walk includes a glass and “12 wine pours from local wineries” in The Village at Totem Lake near the Cinemark, 12600 120th Ave. NE in Kirkland for age 21+.

Event details
